title: Helianthemum lasianthum
taxonomy: Helianthemum lasianthum is a species of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae. It belongs to the genus Helianthemum, which consists of annuals and perennials native to the Mediterranean region. Helianthemum lasianthum is a shrub-like perennial plant that is native to the mountainous regions of southern Europe and western Asia. The classification of Helianthemum lasianthum is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Rosales, and family Cistaceae. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and its position within the plant kingdom.
uses: Helianthemum lasianthum, also known as rockrose, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's leaves and flowers are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. In some cultures, rockrose is used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. Additionally, the plant's extracts have been studied for their potential anticancer and antimicrobial effects. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses of Helianthemum lasianthum.

intro: Helianthemum lasianthum, also known as rockrose or sun rose, is a perennial flowering plant belonging to the family Cistaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which contribute to its potential in treating various health issues such as wounds, digestive problems, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Helianthemum lasianthum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to be responsible for its therapeutic properties.
